block_00: Take a look at the top news of the week
block_03: At the close of trading on 24 August, Tesla’s 3-for-1 split went into effect. It means the number of Tesla shares in circulation increased threefold, which has cut the price of each share by two-thirds, from $891.30 to $297.10. The move is designed to make Tesla stock more accessible to smaller investors, which could broaden the company's shareholder base.
block_07: A cross-border merger between UniCredit Group and Société Générale could resume once the latter CEO steps down next year, according to an analyst from Bank of America. The share price rose almost by 4% this week.
block_10: Analysts believe the U.S. government’s new export restrictions on Nvidia high-end artificial-intelligence products to China could have a lasting negative impact on its business. The share price has fallen by 25% in one week.
